sampleRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample table of contents lists How this works, Detox Pathway Phase I, Detox Pathway Phase II, Results Overview, Gene - SNP Breakdown, Your recommendations, Lab markers to check, and Glossary.
sampleRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed on page 2 of the sample PDF with personal information and disclaimer blocks. This is an observed Detox Pathway sample-report row for output-format parity only; it is not a local detox, lab, supplement, medication, diagnosis, treatment, or actionability conclusion.

sampleRows.item: Phase I and Phase II detox explanation
sampleRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample explains detoxification as a liver-centered multi-step process, describes Phase I CYP enzymes, then Phase II pathways including UGT enzymes, NAT2 acetylation, glutathione, and GSTP1.
sampleRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed on page 3. The page links variants in CYP1A1, CYP2E1, CYP1B1, CYP2D6, UGT1A1, NAT2, and GSTP1 to environmental or chemical sensitivity examples with reference markers. This is an observed Detox Pathway sample-report row for output-format parity only; it is not a local detox, lab, supplement, medication, diagnosis, treatment, or actionability conclusion.

sampleRows.item: Phase I and Phase II pathway diagrams
sampleRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample includes Phase I and Phase II pathway maps with legends for typical, severe, not-determined, upregulating, downregulating, neutral, genotype-not-determined, gene interactions, enhancers, toxins, detox components, lab-result states, and missing lab results.
sampleRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed on rendered pages 4 and 5. The maps connect toxin categories, gene nodes, detox components, and lab-result symbols. This is an observed Detox Pathway sample-report row for output-format parity only; it is not a local detox, lab, supplement, medication, diagnosis, treatment, or actionability conclusion.

sampleRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample results overview states: Predisposed to typical detox ability.

sampleRows.geneticAnalysis: Visible Phase I rows include ADH1B rs1229984 TC; PON1 rs662 TT and rs854560 TA; CYP2C19 rs4244285 GG and rs4986893 GG; CYP1A2 rs762551 AA; CYP1B1 rs1056836 GG; AHR rs2066853 GG; CYP1A1 rs4646903 AA and rs1048943 TT; CYP3A4 rs55785340 AA and rs2242480 CC; CYP2B6 rs3745274 TG; ALDH2 rs671 GG; CYP2A6 rs1801272 AA; CYP2C9 rs1799853 CC and rs1057910 AA.
sampleRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed in the page 6 Phase I Gene - SNP Summary table. This is an observed Detox Pathway sample-report row for output-format parity only; it is not a local detox, lab, supplement, medication, diagnosis, treatment, or actionability conclusion.

sampleRows.item: Phase I lab and health-report chips

sampleRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed on page 6 below the Phase I SNP table. This is an observed Detox Pathway sample-report row for output-format parity only; it is not a local detox, lab, supplement, medication, diagnosis, treatment, or actionability conclusion.

sampleRows.geneticAnalysis: The ADH1B detail page shows SNP rs1229984 ADH1B*2, sample genotype TC, allele notes for C as typical ADH1B activity and T as increased ADH1B activity and altered detox ability, and a sample interpretation that the genotype is linked to increased ADH1B activity and altered detox ability.
sampleRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed on page 8 with intro text about alcohol metabolism and acetaldehyde. This is an observed Detox Pathway sample-report row for output-format parity only; it is not a local detox, lab, supplement, medication, diagnosis, treatment, or actionability conclusion.

folateContext.plainEnglishMeaning: The available local MTHFR calls can be described as folate-processing context, not as a Detox Pathway measurement or toxin-sensitivity result.
folateContext.folicAcidSafetyNote: Under the CDC framing used by this package, common MTHFR variants are not a reason to avoid folic acid.
